content.problemStatement:
Having already mastered cooking, Chef has now decided to learn how to play the guitar. Often while trying to play a song, Chef has to skip several strings to reach the string he has to pluck. Eg. he may have to pluck the 1st1^{st}1st string and then the 6th6^{th}6th string. This is easy in guitars with only 666 strings; However, Chef is playing a guitar with 10610^6106 strings. In order to simplify his task, Chef wants you to write a program that will tell him the total number of strings he has to skip while playing his favourite song.

This is how guitar strings are numbered (In ascending order from right to left). Eg. to switch from string 111 to 666, Chef would have to skip 444 strings (2,3,4,5)(2, 3, 4, 5)(2,3,4,5).

Input:
First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, the number of times Chef has to pluck a string
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers - S1S_1S1​, S2S_2S2​, ..., SNS_NSN​, where SiS_iSi​ is the number of the ithi^{th}ith string Chef has to pluck.
Output:For each testcase, output the total number of strings Chef has to skip over while playing his favourite song.
constraints: 2 ≤ N ≤ 10^5 (total N over all test cases ≤ 10^5)
Si ≠ Si+1 for all valid i
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of strings Chef plucks.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers S1, S2, ..., SN, where Si is the index of the i‑th string (1 ≤ Si ≤ 10^6) and Si ≠ Si+1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total number of strings Chef has to skip while playing the song.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: skips = (|1-6|-1)+( |6-11|-1)+( |11-6|-1)+( |6-10|-1)+( |10-11|-1) = 4+4+4+3+0 = 15.\nTest case 2: each gap is 2, so each contributes 1. Three gaps give 3.

solution.approach:
For each consecutive pair (Si, Si+1) Chef skips all strings strictly between them. The count of skipped strings is |Si - Si+1| - 1. Summing this value over all N‑1 adjacent pairs gives the answer. The algorithm scans the array once, accumulating the sum.

Algorithm:
1. Read T.
2. Loop over test cases:
   a. Read N.
   b. Read the N integers.
   c. Initialise ans = 0.
   d. For i from 0 to N‑2, ans += abs(a[i] - a[i+1]) - 1.
   e. Output ans.
The scan is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total N) – each integer is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input array (which can be processed on the fly).
